title: Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Colgate-Palmolive (CL) compiled by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 4% growth rate reflects the mature nature of the global oral care market, balanced by stronger growth in the Hill's Pet Nutrition segment and ongoing pricing power.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A low 7% discount rate is justified by Colgate's extreme cash flow predictability, defensive product portfolio, and low beta, making it a very low-risk equity.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate is slightly below global GDP growth, appropriate for a mature, massive consumer staples company.

thesisParagraphs: Colgate-Palmolive represents the quintessential defensive stock. It produces essential items that consumers purchase regardless of economic conditions. The crown jewel is its global toothpaste dominance, but the true growth engine is Hill's Pet Nutrition, which capitalizes on the secular trend of pet humanization and premiumization.

However, this extreme safety comes at a premium. The current valuation leaves little room for error and assumes flawless execution in passing on inflationary costs. While it remains a cornerstone holding for dividend growth investors, the DCF model suggests it trades at a slight premium to its intrinsic value, lacking a margin of safety for new capital.
verdictFooter: Based on projected actuals. Data via Finviz (Mar 2026).

faq.question: Why is the growth rate only 4%?
faq.answer: Colgate operates in highly penetrated, mature markets. Significant volume growth is difficult, meaning growth relies primarily on pricing and the faster-growing pet segment.

faq.question: Is the dividend safe?
faq.answer: Yes. Colgate has paid uninterrupted dividends since 1895 and possesses the free cash flow generation to comfortably maintain and grow its payout.

faq.question: Why a 7% discount rate?
faq.answer: The low discount rate reflects the company's defensive nature, low volatility, and highly predictable cash flows, representing lower risk for investors.
